validation_summary.evidence: python -m py_compile exits 0 (~0.1s); python -m unittest skills.development-harness.scripts.tests.test_checkpoint_template -v passes 4/4 (0.001s); python -m unittest discover skills/development-harness/scripts/tests passes 210/210 (33.6s, up from 206 -- 4 new cases for unit_041)

checkpoint.summary: unit_041 complete (PHASE_010 started). Extended skills/development-harness/templates/checkpoint-template.md with a new 'Batch (current or last)' second-level heading carrying four placeholders -- {{BATCH_ID_OR_NONE}}, {{FLEET_MODE}}, {{BATCH_UNIT_ROWS_OR_NONE}}, {{BATCH_CONFLICTS_OR_NONE}} -- plus a per-unit markdown table with the six columns that already exist on state.execution.fleet.units (Unit, Phase, Status, Branch, Started, Ended). The section reflects state.execution.fleet directly, so no reshape is needed when an agent or script renders the checkpoint. A guidance paragraph documents the idle-with-no-prior-batch render. Grep confirmed no runtime script programmatically substitutes these placeholders -- the template is seed material for create.md's initial checkpoint, so the additive edit is safe. New test_checkpoint_template.py module with 4 cases pins the heading, each placeholder, each table column, and -- importantly -- that all 7 pre-PHASE_010 placeholders survive the edit (guard against accidental deletion in future template edits). Suite 206 -> 210. PHASE_009 merged as PR #33, squashed into main at 4a02920; new branch feat/phase-010-observability cut and pushed.
checkpoint.next_action: Complete unit_042 (PHASE_010): update skills/development-harness/commands/state.md and skills/development-harness/templates/workspace-commands/harness-state.md to render three things -- (1) fleet status from state.execution.fleet (reuse the Batch section shape from unit_041); (2) orphaned worktrees via sync_harness.py --dry-run (already implemented in PHASE_006 timeframe); (3) per-batch timings (dispatch start, merge complete, total wall-clock) -- likely derived from .harness/logs/<batch_id>/ once unit_043 lands, but unit_042 can at least specify the timing format ('Batch <id>: dispatched HH:MM:SS, merged HH:MM:SS, total Ns'). Validation: grep both docs + structural presence test in the test module.
